Si Toulouse est aujourd’hui surnommée la: Toulouse owes its nickname, the Pink City, to the Romans. It was during the Pax Romana that the city — then called Palladia Tolosa — inherited the tile and brickwork techniques that still shape its architectural identity. A trading crossroads between the British Isles and the Mediterranean world, it grew rich in temples, ramparts and amphitheatres, some of which can still be seen today, notably the Purpan arena and the collections of the Saint-Raymond museum.
Situé à proximité immédiate du Zénith de: A short distance from the Zénith de Toulouse, the Hôtel Palladia is the ideal base for concerts, shows and events. After the performance, you return to a spacious room, quiet surroundings and free parking. With the promotional code ZENITH2026, you receive 20% off your room on show nights at the Zénith de Toulouse.
Situé proche du cœur de Toulouse, L’Hôte: Close to the centre of Toulouse, the Hôtel Palladia enjoys a strategic position. Easily reached from Toulouse-Blagnac airport and the city centre, it suits local and international delegates alike.
